# Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA)

Jurisdiction: South Africa
Region: ZA
Citation: Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013
Authority: South African Government / Information Regulator
Source URL: https://www.gov.za/sites/default/files/gcis_document/201409/3706726-11act4of2013protectionofpersonalinforcorrect.pdf
Topics: lawful_basis, notice_transparency, consent, sensitive_data, data_subject_rights, third_party_sharing, cross_border_transfer, retention_deletion, security_breach, children_data, marketing_opt_out, data_minimization, accountability
Keywords: POPIA, POPI Act, South Africa, responsible party, operator, special personal information, Information Regulator, 南非

## Summary
South Africa's personal information law with eight processing conditions, data subject rights, direct marketing limits, cross-border transfer rules, information officer duties, and regulator oversight.

## Key compliance obligations
- Meet conditions for lawful processing including accountability, processing limitation, purpose specification, further processing limitation, information quality, openness, security safeguards, and data subject participation.
- Use operator contracts and security safeguards.
- Control special personal information and children's data.
- Meet cross-border transfer and direct marketing requirements.
